# Code of Conduct

## Our Commitment

This project is maintained as a professional portfolio and educational resource for the cybersecurity community. Everyone who participates — whether by opening issues, submitting pull requests, suggesting improvements, or engaging in discussions — is expected to contribute to a respectful, harassment-free environment regardless of experience level, background, identity, or personal characteristics.

---

## Expected Behavior

- Provide constructive, specific, and actionable feedback on documentation, detection logic, or configurations.
- Respect differing technical opinions and approaches — there are many valid ways to architect detection rules, write runbooks, and structure SIEM deployments.
- Accept constructive criticism professionally and use it to improve the project.
- Credit original sources when referencing external detection logic, MITRE ATT&CK mappings, or community resources.

---

## Unacceptable Behavior

- Harassment, personal attacks, or discriminatory language of any kind.
- Publishing others' private information (doxing), including credentials, IP addresses, or infrastructure details discovered through this project's documentation.
- Using detection rules, runbooks, or attack simulation procedures documented in this project to target systems without explicit authorization from the system owner.
- Trolling, inflammatory comments, or sustained disruption of discussions.

---

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies to all interactions within this repository's issue tracker, pull requests, discussions, and any public spaces where an individual is representing this project.

---

## Enforcement

Instances of unacceptable behavior can be reported by contacting the project maintainer through [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/alejandroZ345/-Cloud-Native-Security-Operations-Wazuh-SIEM-XDR-Deployment/issues) or via the contact information listed in the repository's README.

The maintainer will review all reports and take appropriate action, which may include removing comments, closing issues, or blocking participants from future interaction with the project.

---

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant v2.1](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/), the most widely adopted code of conduct for open source projects.
